name: Italy (2003–2006)

colors.symbolism: the Italian national colours; the deeper 2003 Pantone shade

description: A green, white and red vertical tricolour of three equal bands, in the colour shades set by the 2003 Pantone specification (a deeper green and red than the traditional version).
history: Between 2003 and 2006 the Italian tricolour used the colours set by a 2003 government circular specifying Pantone textile values, which produced noticeably darker shades than the traditional flag and provoked public controversy. The colours were re-standardised by law in 2006 (Fern Green, Bright White, Flame Scarlet), replacing this short-lived version.
